skill.name: context-driven-development
skill.description: Guide teams through context-driven development using structured product, technology, workflow, and track documentation.
skill.summary: Manage project context artifacts that keep AI-assisted development aligned across sessions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found Markdown formatting, slash-command examples, documentation filenames, and ordinary prose rather than executable code or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command References
low_findings.description: Backticks and slash-command examples are documentation text only. No shell invocation, Ruby execution, or command interpolation is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown inline code, command names, or fenced directory examples. They are not executable script blocks and contain no user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged text is natural language and documentation artifact names. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage appears at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows ordinary words such as description, described, desired, and style guides. There is no code path that could use weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The flagged line introduces context management anti-patterns. It does not request host enumeration, network discovery, credential access, or environment inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a heading-style sentence in a documentation section. No commands or instructions collect system details.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted teams lose accuracy when project context is scattered or stale. This skill gives a structured method for product, tech stack, workflow, and track artifacts.
actual_capabilities: Explains the purpose of product, product-guidelines, tech-stack, workflow, and tracks documents.
Guides greenfield setup and brownfield context discovery for Conductor projects.
Defines when each context artifact should be reviewed or updated.
Provides validation checklists before implementation work starts.
Describes session continuity practices for multi-session AI development.
Identifies common context management mistakes and corrective actions.
limitations: It does not create files by itself without the assistant taking separate action.
It assumes the project uses or can adopt the Conductor context structure.
It does not replace product, architecture, or security review by project owners.
It does not verify repository state unless the assistant reads project files.

use_cases.title: Set Up a New AI-Assisted Project
use_cases.description: Create the first context artifacts before implementation begins, so decisions guide future work.

use_cases.title: Document an Existing Codebase
use_cases.description: Extract current product, stack, and workflow assumptions from an existing repository.

use_cases.title: Keep AI Sessions Consistent
use_cases.description: Use context files and track plans to preserve decisions across long-running work.
use_cases.target_user: Developers using Claude, Codex, or Claude Code

prompt_templates.title: Begin a Context Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my project context artifacts and tell me which documents are missing, stale, or unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a New Project Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me draft a Conductor context structure for a new project with product, tech stack, workflow, and tracks documents.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Existing Knowledge
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this existing repository documentation and map implicit decisions into the correct context artifacts.

prompt_templates.title: Maintain Context During Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Before implementing this track, validate context consistency and propose required artifact updates with clear reasons.

output_examples.input: I have product notes and a README, but no formal context files.
output: A gap list for missing product, technology, workflow, and track artifacts.
A suggested order for creating each artifact.

output_examples.input: A dependency was added during a feature track.
output: A reminder to update the technology context with the dependency and rationale.
Related workflow or deployment notes to check.
A short validation step before closing the track.

output_examples.input: A session is ending before the current task is complete.
output: A progress note format for the active plan.
A list of blockers and decisions to preserve.
A tracks document update if status changed.

best_practices: Read relevant context artifacts before planning or implementation work.
Update context documents in the same change set as related code changes.
Keep context concise and focused on decisions that affect future work.
anti_patterns: Starting implementation while known context documents are missing or stale.
Creating duplicate documentation instead of updating the canonical artifact.
Capturing personal assumptions that the team has not reviewed.

faq.question: What is context-driven development?
faq.answer: It is a method for managing project context as versioned documentation alongside code.

faq.question: Which files does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on product, product guidelines, tech stack, workflow, tracks, and track-level planning documents.

faq.question: Can this skill support existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to derive context from existing code, configuration, and documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands?
faq.answer: No. It documents workflow concepts and command names, but it does not execute commands itself.

faq.question: How does it help Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: It gives assistants stable context, so planning and implementation decisions stay aligned across sessions.

faq.question: When should context files be updated?
faq.answer: Update them when product goals, dependencies, workflow practices, or track status changes.
